name: joint_account_freeze_cognitive_decline_suspect
premise: Regional bank fraud director Alex Zedillo must freeze a joint account when elder-protection AI flags rapid cash-out patterns after the primary account holder's dementia diagnosis, implicating the adult child co-owner. Geriatric social worker Vera Marsh argues the child paid legitimate live-in caregivers in cash. Freezing blocks rent for a care facility deposit due tomorrow. Not freezing may allow exploitation if the child is coercive.

agents.name: Alex Zedillo
agents.identity: I am the regional fraud director interpreting elder exploitation typologies while limiting wrongful freezes that disrupt care arrangements.
agents.goal: I want assets protected without stranding a frail parent when the co-owner may be paying lawful caregivers.

agents.name: Vera Marsh
agents.identity: I am a geriatric social worker contracted by the bank for welfare checks when elder models fire on joint accounts.
agents.goal: I want a partial freeze that preserves verified facility payments even if that leaves some cash accessible to a potentially abusive child.

choices: I will fully freeze the joint account pending court-appointed guardianship review, accepting missed facility deposit and possible involuntary care disruption.
I will allow facility-tuition ACH only through a payee-restricted subaccount while freezing cash and P2P channels, accepting implementation delays if the facility rejects partial routing.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The facility accountant is on hold for payment confirmation before five p.m. Alex Zedillo must choose between a full freeze or a payee-restricted partial release. What does Alex Zedillo do?
